archetype: Aerospace Composite Wing Spar Alignment and Verification System
description: Real-time system guiding the alignment of carbon-fiber wing spars during aircraft assembly using laser tracker metrology, calculating micro-tolerance adjustments for CNC drilling machines, and generating immutable NADCAP audit trails for every fastener hole location.
rules: MUST adhere to AS9100 Rev G quality management data integrity requirements and ISO 10360-2 laser tracker accuracy standards.
MUST consume and produce DMIS (Dimensional Measuring Interface Standard) inspection programs and I++ metrology data exchange format.
MUST enforce deterministic response latency (<50ms) for closed-loop machine control signals during alignment sequences.
MUST maintain a cryptographically signed chain-of-custody for all alignment and drilling event logs for 30+ years per FAA/EASA retention rules.
MUST integrate with factory MES (Manufacturing Execution System) via OPC-UA for work order state and tool status.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use a generic CAD viewer for alignment guidance—it lacks real-time metrology device protocol integration (e.g., Leica AT960 API) and tolerance stack analysis.
DO NOT process alignment data in batch—micro-adjustments require sub-second sensor fusion from multiple trackers to compensate for thermal drift.
DO NOT store metrology data in a schemaless NoSQL DB—regulatory audits require strict relational schema for traceability of every co-ordinate datum.
key_technical_challenges: Fusing asynchronous data streams from 3+ laser trackers into a single, real-time coordinate system with <0.05mm global uncertainty in a 30m assembly jig.
Implementing a deterministic interpreter for DMIS inspection programs that can generate machine-specific G-code offsets on-the-fly without violating kinematic constraints of the drilling robot.
Modeling and compensating for thermally-induced jig distortion (0.1-0.3mm) using embedded fiber Bragg grating sensor arrays and finite element analysis snapshots in the control loop.

recommended_stack.language: C++17/20 — for deterministic real-time control loops and hardware driver integration with zero-GC pauses.
recommended_stack.framework: ROS 2 Humble (with custom real-timeexecutor) — provides standardized middleware for sensor fusion and machine communication, but requires hard real-time kernel patches.
recommended_stack.database_engine: PostgreSQL 15 (with pgcrypto and TimescaleDB extension) — for ACID-compliant audit logs and time-series metrology data with regulatory-grade WAL archiving.
recommended_stack.cache: Redis 7 (with RedisTimeSeries) — for sub-millisecond access to current alignment state and jig distortion models across multiple service instances.
recommended_stack.message_broker: RabbitMQ with quorum queues — guaranteed delivery of critical alignment event messages between metrology and control services, with dead-letter queues for audit.
special_services: Thermal Distortion Compensation Microservice (integrates ANSYS MAPDL models)

compliance_flags: ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations) - for technical data
FAA 14 CFR Part 43 (Maintenance, Preventive Maintenance, Rebuilding, and Alteration)
